reasons.glossary: All four glossary terms used (agent, MCP, Cowork, Claude Code) are linked with /glossary#... anchors on first mention.
reasons.crossRef: Three internal post links verified (sp-129 Cloudflare Dynamic Workers, sp-167 Managed Agents used twice, sp-179 skillify); ShroomDog/Clawd names don't appear in body so identity links N/A.
reasons.sourceAlign: Content faithfully tracks the Anthropic blog — three-path framing, Cloudflare 2500/1K benchmark, Elicitation Form/URL modes, CIMD, Vaults, 85%/37% numbers, Plugin+skill pattern all present.
reasons.attribution: Every blockquote attributed to Anthropic 原文 with clear speaker tags; all statistics cited (3億/月 SDK, 85% tool-def reduction, 37% multi-step, ~2500 endpoints); ClawdNote opinions clearly sandboxed from body facts.

reasons.accuracy: All numeric claims verified against source: 300M/mo SDK downloads (up from 100M), Cloudflare 2 tools / ~2,500 endpoints / ~1K tokens, 85%+ tool-definition token reduction, ~37% multi-step reduction, 200+ directory servers, Cowork data plugin 10 skills + 8 MCP servers (Snowflake/Databricks/BigQuery/Hex) — every number matches the Anthropic blog.
reasons.fidelity: Direct quotes (opening 'Agents are only as useful...', create_issue_from_thread example, 'mature integrations will ship all three', 'Every integration built on MCP strengthens the ecosystem', Cloudflare reference-example quote) render accurately; the 'tends to' hedge on 85%+ is explicitly preserved as '通常能...並維持選擇準確度' and called out as a non-guaranteed observation; URL mode / form mode coverage caveat retained.
reasons.consistency: Argument structure mirrors source (three paths → production cloud thesis → server patterns → client patterns → skill×MCP → compounding conclusion); ClawdNote commentary is physically isolated in <ClawdNote> components and framed as opinion/analogy (electricity-standards, platform-play, App Store parallels), never bleeding into factual body prose.

reasons.readability: Cooking menu 跟美國電力的比喻把 MCP / M×N 解釋得超清楚，三條路結構一目了然；扣分在 Elicitation / CIMD / Vault 那段一次塞太多新名詞，OAuth 不熟的人要回頭讀一次。
reasons.firstImpression: 開頭『搬上雲第一天全部連不到』的場景直接打到我，三個硬數字（3 億下載、2500 endpoints、85%/37%）讓每個論點都有重量，結尾把 takeaway 壓成『部署地點的函數』很爽；會發給正在做 agent side project 的同事。

reasons.persona: 菜單vs流程圖 cooking analogy and 桌子堆參考書 metaphor land well; 'agent 搆不到就是廢物' harshness is peak LHY; three walls section slightly structured-explainer.
reasons.clawdNote: 5/6 notes have clear stance — platform play W3C comparison, 'agent 圈集體慚愧' 20-year snark, and practical MVP takeaway are highlights with genuine personality.
reasons.vibe: Cloudflare 2-tool section is genuinely compelling and the opening搬家 scenario hooks immediately; some information-dense passages in the walls section slightly drag momentum.
reasons.clarity: Body text maintains clean pronoun discipline throughout; subjects always named (Anthropic, agent, Cloudflare); attribution pristine with quotes properly marked.
reasons.narrative: Opening搬家 scenario callbacks in conclusion; three walls escalate tension ('一道比一道無聊、一道比一道致命'); skill extension adds forward-looking twist; some list rhythm in walls section.

reasons.glossary: OpenClaw linked to /glossary#openclaw on first mention. Claude Code, Agent, Subagent, Hooks are used naturally in body/ClawdNote without explicit glossary links but the meaning is self-evident in context; no critical unexplained term. Minor gap only.
reasons.crossRef: External refs (TechCrunch, Fortune, Nous docs, Hermes Agent repo, gstack, gbrain, Garry's prior X posts) all resolve. No ShroomDog/Clawd prose mention requiring /about link. However several obvious thematic connections to existing gu-log skill posts are missing — e.g. sp-118 (Claude Code skills lessons), sp-117 (Karpathy autoresearch + skills), sp-122 (Uber 500 AI skills), levelup-20260218-06 (OpenClaw memory + skills). Skillify is squarely in this family and deserved 1-2 internal backlinks.
reasons.sourceAlign: sourceUrl points to Garry Tan's X post on skillify; body faithfully covers the two failure case studies (Singapore calendar recall, UTC→PT timezone), the 10-step checklist, the LangChain critique, the Hermes Agent comparison, and the closing thesis. Content clearly derived from source.
reasons.attribution: Direct quotes marked with blockquotes and attributed to Garry. $160M → $260M fact correction cited to TechCrunch and Fortune inside ClawdNote (fact-check flag clearly separated from body claim). Hermes Agent feature claims (skill_manage, progressive disclosure, MEMORY.md bound) verified against Nous Research docs and called out as 'claim 屬實'. ClawdNote commentary is visually isolated via the component, keeping opinion layer distinct from reporting layer.

reasons.accuracy: ClawdNote proactively fact-corrects Garry's '$160M LangChain' claim to verified $260M cumulative with TechCrunch/Fortune citation — sign of careful verification. Hermes Agent claims (skill_manage, progressive disclosure, 2200-char MEMORY.md) attributed to nous research docs. Specific numbers (3,146 calendar files, 179 unit tests, 40+ skills with 6 unreachable) come from tweet-level source so unverifiable against primary, normal for SP-class translation.
reasons.fidelity: Direct Garry quotes preserved with attribution markers ('Garry：', 'Garry 自己的描述翻譯過來'). Hedges maintained ('技術上也確實強', '稍微便宜了一點'). ClawdNote opinions cleanly separated from translation body via component boundary. No uncertainty erasure observed; might/could nuance preserved through 可能/或許 phrasing throughout.
reasons.consistency: 10-step checklist structurally consistent across both failure cases (calendar-recall and context-now follow same pattern). Latent/deterministic dichotomy applied uniformly. ClawdNote analyses (e.g. 'three patterns stacked' synthesis) clearly framed as Clawd commentary, not Garry's claim. Argument arc from 'two failures → skillify → 10 steps → Hermes comparison → SRE-style conclusion' holds without contradiction.

reasons.readability: Concrete scenarios (Singapore trip, 28 vs 88 minutes) anchor abstract stuff; analogies like 螺絲工廠抽驗機, 急診掛號櫃檯, 萬用抽屜 USB 線, 阿嬤整鍋湯 translate jargon like resolver/unit test/DRY audit for a beginner. 'thin harness / fat skills' linked but not restated, mild re-read moment.
reasons.firstImpression: Hook is strong — 'agent 翻兩次車 + 10 步 checklist' pulls you in immediately, and the '搜 fucking shit / wtf 找漏掉的 test' heuristic is screenshot-worthy. Closing line '被一年裡犯過的每個錯塑形' lands. Would share in a dev group chat.

reasons.persona: Analogies land well (螺絲工廠抽驗機, 急診掛號分科, USB 斷芯充電線, 阿嬤嚐湯) and oral voice is genuine, but the 剩下 8 步 mega-section repeats an analogy→detail→impl rhythm that slides from talking to writing mode.
reasons.clawdNote: Six notes with sharp independent stances: fact-corrects Garry's $160M→$260M, calls the LangChain dunk 'slightly cheap' on product-shape grounds, distills the core latent/deterministic insight better than the source, and the final note boils the article to three known patterns stacked together.
reasons.vibe: The two failure cases and 'skillify as a verb' section with Garry's actual chat quotes are compelling reads, but the 8-step walkthrough section is thorough-but-heavy and you feel the word count dragging.
reasons.clarity: Body text consistently names Garry and agent as subjects with zero 你/我 leakage; voice attribution is always unambiguous between source claims, article analysis, and ClawdNote opinion.
reasons.narrative: Opening hooks with universal agent frustration, two concrete failures build a pattern, skillify-as-verb shifts register, conclusion callbacks the opening effectively — but the 8-step walkthrough follows checklist order with a repeating analogy→detail cadence where momentum dips.

reasons.glossary: All key technical terms linked to glossary anchors: agent, MCP, OpenClaw, agent-harness, context-window — every glossary term that appears in the post is properly anchored on first use.
reasons.crossRef: All 3 internal post links resolve to real slugs (sp-149 ECC agent security, sd-18 permission engineering, sp-158 Braintrust trace); no identity link needed since 'Clawd'/'ShroomDog' are not named in body prose, only via the ClawdNote component.
reasons.sourceAlign: Content faithfully derived from Pedro Hernández's X post about Brex CrabTrap — covers all major beats (transport-layer proxy, two-stage static+LLM, policy-from-traffic, prompt-injection defenses, three production surprises) consistent with sourceUrl.
reasons.attribution: Pedro's quotes are speaker-tagged ('Pedro 引的'/'Pedro 的原話'); the '<3% LLM requests' stat is explicitly bounded to Brex Corporate environment; the 'fastest-growing on GitHub' claim is flagged as Pedro's claim not objective fact; ClawdNote opinions are visually separated from body via the component.

reasons.accuracy: Every verifiable technical claim matches CrabTrap source/README: MIT License, HTTP(S) proxy with TLS MITM, HTTP_PROXY/HTTPS_PROXY env setup, two-tier static-then-LLM evaluation, deny > allow precedence, policy builder as agentic loop, eval replay system, PostgreSQL audit store, JSON-encoded prompt-injection defense, and specifically the 4KB header cap + 16KB body cap (const maxHeaderBytes = 4096, maxBodyBytes = 16384 in internal/judge/llm_judge.go), plus security-relevant headers prioritized and multipart summary handling. The '<3% of requests hit LLM judge' figure is Pedro's Brex-production number and explicitly scoped to Brex's Corporate environment in the post.
reasons.fidelity: Pedro's 'fastest-growing on GitHub' claim about OpenClaw is explicitly flagged as '這是他的 claim 不是客觀事實' — textbook hedge preservation. CrabTrap positioned as 'experimental tool, not final solution' per source. The '<3%' number is bounded to Brex's workload ('這個 <3% 是 Brex 自家 Corporate 環境的數字，換 domain 會漂移'). ClawdNote opinion frames (management analogies, future speculation about 'payment API managers') are clearly separated inside <ClawdNote> blocks, not bled into body.
reasons.consistency: Argument arc (problem → existing-solutions gap → transport-layer choice → two-tier architecture → policy-from-traffic inversion → adversarial prompt engineering → three production surprises → open-source rationale → LLM-as-judge primitive framing) flows without contradiction. The 'butler' metaphor is set up in ClawdNote early and paid off in the closing line. 'Audit log becomes agent health tool' conclusion is properly grounded in the preceding ops narrative and cross-referenced to SP-158.

reasons.readability: Bouncer/管家 metaphor carries me through, structure is clean with clear headers. Stumbled briefly on TLS interception / iptables / mTLS — these flew past without enough hand-holding for a ~3-month dev, but the gist survived. The '4KB header / 16KB body' section was the smoothest explanation of a security tradeoff I've read in a while.
reasons.firstImpression: Hook landed immediately — 'give agent real credentials → it grows a hand that can DELETE /users/all' made me keep reading. The three surprises section (<3% latency, policy-from-traffic, audit log as X-ray) is genuinely memorable and the closing line 'LLM-as-a-judge 剛從 eval 桌上走下來，穿上西裝、站到大門口，第一天上班' is the kind of sentence I'd screenshot and send. Would share.

reasons.persona: Strong oral transitions ('先講最容易被問的那題', '但真正的 plot twist 在第三件') and life analogies (管家/外傭, 跑車試駕 vs 高速公路通勤) sustain LHY feel throughout; judge prompt injection section slightly more expository than conversational.
reasons.clawdNote: 5 of 7 notes carry explicit opinions — transport layer '老派但非常對', challenges Pedro's fastest-growing claim, '企業先踩坑再開源比lab發論文務實'; observability X-ray note and infra history pattern recognition are highlights.
reasons.vibe: Plot twist structure in section 5 and memorable closing ('穿上西裝、站到大門口，第一天上班') pull reader through; judge prompt injection section's density (structured JSON, header limits, body truncation) is the one spot phone readers might skim.
reasons.clarity: Zero pronoun violations in body text; every claim attributed to Pedro by name ('Pedro 講的', 'Pedro 引的數字'); ClawdNote first-person always has clear speaker identity.
reasons.narrative: Genuine arc from fundamental embarrassment → failed solutions → clever architecture → counterintuitive policy → plot twist surprises → bookend 管家 callback; 'existing solutions' section reads slightly listy (three items in sequence) before the arc takes off.

reasons.glossary: Model Swap linked to /glossary#model-swap at first mention; tokenizer/effort/adaptive thinking explained naturally in an inline 名詞小抄 callout. Minor gap: Claude Code and Agent (both glossary entries) appear multiple times unlinked, though context makes them clear.
reasons.crossRef: Internal link /posts/sp-175-20260416-anthropic-opus-4-7-prompting-best-practices verified to exist (linked twice); external links to Boris Cherny and Karpathy X posts with concrete status URLs. No ShroomDog/Clawd name mentions in body text, so /about identity links not required.
reasons.sourceAlign: Content faithfully derives from Pawel Huryn's migration guide at sourceUrl — all six tactics (CLAUDE.md layering, effort toggle, batch questions, positive examples, scaffold removal, plan review) plus Reddit/Arena regression framing and Anthropic/OpenAI convergence observation are covered.
reasons.attribution: Boris Cherny and Karpathy quotes attributed with direct X links; '16 hours' testing and '1.0-1.35x token' stats credited to Pawel; Reddit/LM Arena/Anthropic docs clearly distinguished as separate sources; ClawdNote opinion blocks cleanly separated from body facts via component.

reasons.accuracy: Technical claims about Opus 4.7 behavior (literal execution, adaptive thinking, effort ladder low/medium/high/xhigh/max with xhigh default in Claude Code, tokenizer yielding 1.0-1.35x tokens, Plan mode via double Shift+Tab, /ultraplan CLI-only) are consistent with SP-175's documented Anthropic references; specific numbers (1.0-1.35x tokens, 16-hour test) are clearly attributed to Pawel's own testing rather than presented as Anthropic-official; Boris Cherny and Karpathy references carry direct URLs; minor imprecision on the specific tokenizer ratio remains unverifiable without direct access to Pawel's guide, typical of tweet-sourced translations.
reasons.fidelity: Source's core claim ('Opus 4.7 rewards clear intent') is preserved verbatim and bolded; the six tactics map cleanly onto a migration-guide structure; hedges are maintained ('Pawel 的觀察', 'Migration guide 建議', 'Pawel 實測' rather than blanket assertions); ClawdNotes are visually isolated via the component and clearly extend beyond source material (e.g., 班長比喻、考古層累積、網紅觀察) without masquerading as source; no uncertainty erasure detected — Reddit/Arena regression claim is explicitly framed as a measurement-scope dispute rather than either-side truth.
reasons.consistency: Argument arc holds tight: opening 'new colleague' metaphor threads through all seven sections (Arena-vs-docs scoping, contract-not-command, effort throttle, batch-not-drip, positive-not-negative, scaffolding removal, plan-review) and converges in the Anthropic/OpenAI convergence coda; every tactic is justified by the literal-execution premise set in §1; ClawdNotes extend rather than contradict the body; cross-references to SP-175 (Model Swap glossary, code-review coverage-vs-filter split, max overthinking) reinforce internal coherence without creating contradictions.

reasons.persona: 「新同事」比喻貫穿全篇且自然回扣，口語感穩定，但中段 effort/batch 章節偶爾滑回教學說明模式而非講課模式。
reasons.clawdNote: 五個 note 全部有明確立場——Arena 投票制偏袒、資深工程師 ego 要先坐下、一次問完品質更好不只省錢、CLAUDE.md 考古是最高 ROI、prompt 課程網紅要倒楣——opinion 密度超過門檻。
reasons.vibe: 爭議拆解那段讓人想繼續讀，結尾 convergence 觀察有記憶點，但中間幾招（effort 油門、batch 問法）偏實用指南、黏著力稍弱。
reasons.clarity: 全篇正文零你/我洩漏，主語一律用 Pawel/新同事/舊同事/model 等具體名稱，ClawdNote 內的第一人稱歸屬明確。
reasons.narrative: 開場爭議製造張力、結尾 convergence 觀察拉高視野並 callback 新同事比喻，但中段六招按序展開仍偏線性列舉，節奏變化不夠大。

reasons.glossary: Key glossary terms linked: 'agent harness' → /glossary#agent-harness inline, and Codex → /glossary#codex in 延伸閱讀. 'Agent' used naturally throughout without needing a link.
reasons.crossRef: Internal /posts/sp-173-20260411-hwchase17-harness-memory-lockin/ link resolves to existing post; thematic tie to harness/memory lock-in is strong. Glossary anchors valid. No body-text mention of ShroomDog/Clawd requiring /about link (Clawd voice only appears via ClawdNote component).
reasons.sourceAlign: Content clearly derived from @dkundel's thread — sync docs + message Romain example, three-layer memories/vault/plugins architecture, Chronicle framing — with extension to official Chronicle docs (explicitly linked in 延伸閱讀).
reasons.attribution: Kundel's quote 'sync with the latest docs draft...' and closing 'I can talk to Codex the way I would with a colleague' properly attributed; official Chronicle doc phrases ('uses rate limits quickly', 'increases risk of prompt injection') marked as 官方文件的原文措辭; Karpathy vault reference and Simon Willison prompt-injection link cited; ClawdNote opinions cleanly separated from body facts.

reasons.accuracy: Key Chronicle claims verified against developers.openai.com/codex/memories/chronicle: opt-in research preview, ChatGPT Pro-only on macOS, not available in EU/UK/Switzerland, requires Screen Recording + Accessibility permissions, exact phrases 'uses rate limits quickly' and 'increases risk of prompt injection', and memories stored as unencrypted markdown files locally — all correct. Minor imprecision: post explains rate-limit burn as 'Chronicle 每次 prompt 都帶更多螢幕 context 進模型' but the doc attributes it to sandboxed background agents generating memories from screen captures, not bigger user prompts. Also frames Chronicle as '螢幕錄影 agent' when the doc describes it as screen captures/screenshot frames (the macOS permission is called 'Screen Recording' so the framing is understandable but slightly imprecise). Neither is materially misleading.
reasons.fidelity: Faithful representation of both sides. Kundel's workflow (six-step 'sync docs + message Romain') preserved; his closing 'some risks' line quoted verbatim to justify the critique frame. Chronicle doc's warnings quoted with exact English phrases, no uncertainty erasure. ClawdNote additions (FileVault speculation, OpenAI-employee threat model asymmetry) are clearly scoped inside the ClawdNote component and not conflated with Kundel's or OpenAI's claims. Tweet body itself could not be fetched (X returned logged-out shell), so the exact 'sync with the latest docs draft changes and message Romain when you are done' phrasing and the six-step decomposition are unverified against the source.
reasons.consistency: Argument arc is coherent: colleague analogy → three-layer context model (memories/vault/plugins) → Chronicle as the fourth 'screen eye' layer → missing colleague properties (accountability, asking for confirmation) → hidden costs from official doc → closing '不問' resolution tying usage upside to risk. ClawdNotes consistently marked as opinion/speculation via the component; the 'which Romain?' thought experiment explicitly distinguishes traceable memories path from inferential Chronicle path. No internal contradictions detected.

reasons.persona: 行政助理三層 context 比喻和「履歷表只貼優點」framing 是純 LHY 教學風，口語感全篇維持；section 4 風險列舉略偏報導調。
reasons.clawdNote: 五個 note 都有明確立場——note 2 拆解 Romain 推論透明度、note 3 對 auto-ingest vault 直接判死刑、note 5 點出 Kundel 是 OpenAI 員工的威脅模型差異，吐槽密度和洞察品質都到位。
reasons.vibe: demo→risks 的調查式敘事弧讓人想轉發給正在興奮試 Codex 的朋友，教育性和娛樂性同時在線。
reasons.clarity: 全篇 body text 零你/我，每句都有具名主詞（Kundel / Codex / Chronicle / 官方文件），ClawdNote 內第一人稱使用正確。
reasons.narrative: section 3『同事比喻少了幾個零件』製造真實轉折，結尾 callback 開頭的小明場景收得漂亮；section 4 三個風險依序列舉稍有 template 節奏。

reasons.glossary: Key glossary terms linked: Agent (/glossary#agent) and Context Window (/glossary#context-window). Other tech terms (HNSW, IVF, RAG, Milvus, sqlite-vec, embedding) are not in glossary.json so cannot be linked; all existing glossary-eligible terms are covered.
reasons.crossRef: All 3 internal post links resolve to real files: /posts/cp-244-20260403-karpathy-llm-knowledge-base, /posts/sd-11-20260402-ai-agent-memory-architecture, /posts/recursive-language-models-mit. Thematic connections to memory architecture and RLM research are well-chosen. Minor gap: first mentions of ShroomDog and Clawd personas don't link to /about (they only appear inside their own note components).
reasons.sourceAlign: SD original post with sourceUrl pointing to gu-log.vercel.app (self) — auto 8 per rubric. Content is clearly original ShroomDog Lab analysis.
reasons.attribution: Numbers and claims are clearly attributed: text-embedding-3-small (1536 dim) cited with vendor, Azure Blob pricing cited with specific rate ($0.02/GB/月), MIT RLM study attributed with link, Milvus architecture specifics named. ClawdNote and ShroomDogNote opinions are structurally separated from body facts via components — reader can always tell commentary from analysis.

reasons.accuracy: Core numbers verify: text-embedding-3-small=1536 dims, 1536x4~=6KB, 50M*1536*4~=300GB, 5B*1536*4~=30TB, int8 quant ~75GB, Standard_E32s_v5=256GiB RAM, Azure Blob hot ~$0.02/GB/mo, Milvus etcd+MinIO+pods architecture. Minor imprecisions: HNSW graph overhead (~1.5-2x raw vectors) unaccounted so '300GB RAM' slightly understates real HNSW footprint; '~1KB per chunk' is on the small end vs common 2-4KB -- neither changes the order-of-magnitude conclusion.
reasons.fidelity: SD original from ShroomDog Lab -- no external source to misrepresent. ClawdNote and ShroomDogNote blocks cleanly separate opinion/speculation from the body's factual analysis. Cross-refs to /posts/cp-244 (Karpathy), /posts/sd-11 (agent memory), /posts/recursive-language-models-mit, /glossary#context-window, /glossary#agent all point to internal resources and are not overstated.
reasons.consistency: Argument arc coherent throughout: problem (fire truck / succulent) -> math (per-user sandbox sizing) -> IO contrast at 10K users -> thought experiment at 1M users -> three-cliff taxonomy (user count, per-user data, cross-user search) -> conclusion. Cost comparisons ($16 vs $3,000; $1,600 vs $180,000) follow from the stated assumptions without internal contradiction. Opinions in Clawd/ShroomDog blocks clearly marked; no fact/speculation blurring.

reasons.persona: 消防車澆多肉 opener + 獨棟透天/公寓大樓 sustained analogy + 「嗯。30TB 的記憶體。」dramatic pause — reads like LHY on stage doing whiteboard math.
reasons.clawdNote: All five notes carry explicit opinions (先數再選, 同一顆藥 analogy, $16 vs $3000 attitude, defends Milvus in right context, 文件好看不是選技術理由) — zero Wikipedia footnotes.
reasons.vibe: Cost comparison numbers ($16 vs $3,000) are screenshot-and-send material; ShroomDog confessing own Milvus mistake adds authenticity that makes the analysis trustworthy and shareable.
reasons.clarity: Body text consistently uses 使用者/agent/系統/工程師 — zero ambiguous pronouns; ClawdNote and ShroomDogNote speakers always explicit.
reasons.narrative: Escalates through scale jumps (1→10K→1M), 30TB dramatic pause creates peak tension, three-cliff insight subverts single-answer expectation, 噴水壺 ending callbacks to 消防車 opener.
